description: Coverage targets for WooCommerce across Store API, REST API, database schema, server requests, and browser requests.

status_contract.public_read_success: Store API catalog GET cases should return 200.
status_contract.public_session_read_success: Store API cart/checkout GET cases should return 200 without credentials.
status_contract.auth_boundary: Woo REST and wc-admin GET cases intentionally run without credentials and should return 401 or 403; 2xx, 404, or 5xx statuses are actionable coverage failures.

cart-session-overwrite-race.coverage_shape: concurrent browser cart session overwrite attempts against the WooCommerce cart session boundary

notes: Uses synthetic cart/session fixture data in an isolated runner
Does not target production stores or external payment providers

checkout-concurrent-create-order.coverage_shape: concurrent shortcode checkout order creation with duplicate-submit and completed-order safety probes

notes: Creates fixture products, carts, and orders only inside the benchmark site
Uses offline payment modes unless explicitly configured by the runner

checkout-gateway-compatibility-matrix.coverage_shape: checkout gateway compatibility matrix across core and configured extension payment profiles

notes: Matrix rows are explicit about blocked credential/account states
Runner configuration controls which external gateway plugins are mounted

checkout-shortcode-place-order-latency.coverage_shape: shortcode checkout place-order latency with large synthetic cart and historical-order fixture pressure

notes: Uses generated products, variations, and orders in the isolated benchmark database
Payment methods are constrained by benchmark environment variables

checkout-shipping-cache.coverage_shape: shipping package cache churn, warm-run, and rehash behavior for large synthetic carts

notes: Mutates only fixture carts, products, and shipping state in the benchmark site
Does not call live carrier services

admin-dashboard-physical-products-query.coverage_shape: Woo admin dashboard query behavior for synthetic physical-product catalog fixtures

notes: Seeds fixture products and terms in the isolated database
Admin dashboard reads are bounded to benchmark-generated state

layered-nav-count-cache.coverage_shape: layered navigation count cache behavior under generated catalog and attribute-term fixtures

notes: Creates synthetic catalog data in the isolated runner
Does not persist or publish content outside the benchmark site

layered-nav-catalog-crawl.coverage_shape: catalog crawl coverage across layered navigation URLs generated from synthetic products and attributes

notes: Crawls local benchmark URLs only
Fixture generation is limited to the isolated runner database

admin-page-coverage.coverage_shape: bounded authenticated wp-admin and Woo admin menu/submenu GET coverage for administrator and shop manager roles

notes: GET-only admin page visits with explicit skips for create/install/update/destructive screens
Per-role page limits bound the request volume

woocommerce-rest-route-inventory.coverage_shape: registered WooCommerce REST, Store API, wc-admin, and wc-analytics route inventory

notes: Inspects the local REST route registry
Does not execute route handlers

rest-product-batch-import.coverage_shape: WooCommerce REST product batch import behavior for generated products, attributes, terms, and variation-create focus cases

notes: Creates synthetic catalog records in the benchmark database
Image import defaults are disabled by the full-surface rig environment

woocommerce-external-http-guardrail.coverage_shape: marketplace and payment webhook external HTTP guardrail probes with unapproved network requests blocked

notes: Installs the external HTTP guardrail before probes run
Only allowlisted WooCommerce marketplace traffic is permitted; payment webhook probes are expected to be captured or blocked

generated-rest-request-cases.coverage_shape: generated safe WooCommerce Store API, REST API, and admin REST request cases with captured responses

notes: Uses explicit GET request cases against local WooCommerce routes
Response capture is intended for reviewable route-contract evidence

rest-db-query-profile.coverage_shape: DB query profile for safe WooCommerce Store API and REST API request cases

notes: Profiles bounded GET request cases
Samples and query text length are capped to keep artifacts reviewable

db-inventory.coverage_shape: WooCommerce fixture database table, row, column, and index inventory after full-surface setup

notes: Reads database schema and row counts from the isolated benchmark database
Includes columns and indexes for contract review

woocommerce-browser-coverage.coverage_shape: browser request coverage for shop, product, cart, checkout bootstrap, orders admin, products admin, and analytics admin scenarios

notes: Runs against a WP Codebox browser fixture with a synthetic virtual product
Checkout coverage stops at page bootstrap/request coverage rather than payment completion
